idea.hook: Want to visit our closest stellar neighbor?
facts: Proxima Centauri is 4.24 light-years away. That's ~40 TRILLION kilometers!
Humanity's fastest craft (Parker Solar Probe, ~692,000 km/h) would take over 6,600 years to get there.
Even a hypothetical starship at 10% the speed of light would still require a 42-year journey!
idea.payoff: Interstellar travel demands patience on a cosmic scale. We're not there yet!
idea.title: To Our Closest Star? 🚀⏳

scenes.text: Want to visit our closest stellar neighbor?
scenes.visual: View of deep space with a field of stars. A slightly brighter, red dwarf star (Proxima Centauri) is visible in the distance, with a faint, hopeful trajectory line pointing towards it.
Deep space background. The text '4.24 Light-Years' appears. Below it, a number counter rapidly scrolls through digits, then settles on 'That's ~40 TRILLION KM!' in a slightly smaller font.
An illustration of the Parker Solar Probe speeding through space. A digital clock overlay shows years rapidly counting up, stopping at '6,600+ YEARS'.
A sleek, futuristic starship streaks across the screen, leaving a light trail. A calendar animation quickly flips through pages, stopping definitively at '42 Years'.

scenes.text: INTERSTELLAR TRAVEL: A COSMIC WAIT. WE'RE NOT THERE YET!
scenes.visual: The camera pulls back from the distant Proxima Centauri, revealing a small, blue Earth in the foreground. A large, shimmering 'Not Yet' text briefly flashes on screen before fading with the final text.
